title: ENSO Cycle Interactive
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive visualization of Normal, El Nino, and La Nina phases showing trade wind, ocean temperature, and atmospheric circulation interactions

learningObjectives: Explain how trade winds, ocean temperatures, and atmospheric circulation interact during El Nino and La Nina phases
Describe the ecological and weather consequences of each ENSO phase
Identify ENSO as a coupled ocean-atmosphere system with global effects
prerequisites: Basic understanding of ocean currents and wind patterns
Familiarity with the concept of upwelling
Knowledge of how ocean temperature affects weather

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Animated state comparison with regional detail on hover
keyQuestions: What drives the shift from Normal to El Nino conditions?
How does suppressed upwelling affect marine ecosystems?
Why does El Nino cause drought in Australia but flooding in Peru?
How are ENSO events connected to coral bleaching?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think El Nino is simply 'warm weather' rather than a specific ocean-atmosphere pattern
Students may not realize that ENSO affects global weather patterns far beyond the Pacific
Students may confuse ENSO (a natural oscillation) with long-term climate change
assessmentOpportunities: Students complete a three-column comparison chart for Normal, El Nino, and La Nina states
Students research a specific historical ENSO event and connect it to simulation observations
Students explain feedback mechanisms driving ENSO transitions
